steps.title: Identify the structures and their relationship
steps.content: Before the blank is part of an independent clause: “…is less well known than his ______.” After the blank is another independent clause: “it is still considered by scholars to be an important work.” The word “still” signals contrast with “less well known”—these two ideas oppose each other.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A comma + coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses.

Choice A (“paintings, but”) supplies both the comma and the conjunction “but,” which expresses the contrast the passage requires.

The remaining choices violate the rule:
  • B provides only a comma (comma splice).
  • C provides neither a comma nor a conjunction (fused sentence).
  • D omits the required comma before the conjunction.
That confirms A is correct.
